record.field: Ackee is a self-hosted, privacy-first website analytics tool that gives you clean traffic insights without cookies or invasive tracking.

Ackee is a self-hosted, privacy-first website analytics tool that gives you clean traffic insights without cookies or invasive tracking.

Built on Node.js and MongoDB, Ackee runs entirely on your own server and analyzes the traffic of your websites through a minimal, fast dashboard. It keeps all tracked data anonymized, so visitors are never personally identifiable, and exposes everything through a GraphQL API. It is the right fit for developers and site owners who want meaningful traffic statistics without the complexity of a full marketing platform like Google Analytics or Matomo.

Key features
  • Cookieless, anonymized tracking with a rotating-salt hashing scheme; no consent banner required
  • Views, referrers, durations, devices, browsers and platform breakdowns in a focused, fast UI
  • Optional event tracking for clicks, outbound links, sign-ups and other custom actions
  • Unlimited domains and apps from a single instance
  • Full GraphQL API so every dashboard metric is available programmatically
  • Lightweight self-hosting with Docker, Kubernetes, Vercel, Netlify and manual deployments
Best for

Developers, indie makers and privacy-conscious teams who want GDPR/CCPA-friendly, self-hosted web analytics with full data ownership and an API-first design, and who don't need teams, role-based access or heatmaps.

services: ackee: image: electerious/ackee:3.4.2 ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:3000' environment: - ACKEE_USERNAME={{ ackee_username }} - ACKEE_PASSWORD={{ ackee_password }} - ACKEE_MONGODB={{ ackee_mongodb }} container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network mongo: image: mongo:4 environment: - MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD={{ mongo_initdb_root_password }} - MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME=mongo volumes: - ./mongo_data:/data/db container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_mongo' restart: unless-stopped networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # mongodump con auth si la imagen define MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME (lo habitual): sin # credenciales mongodump falla con «command listDatabases requires authentication». docker compose exec -T mongo sh -c ' if [ -n "${MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME:-}" ]; then exec mongodump --archive -u "$MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME" -p "$MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD" --authenticationDatabase admin fi exec mongodump --archive ' > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.archive" echo "mongodump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.archive") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" docker compose up -d mongo sleep 8 docker compose exec -T mongo sh -c ' if [ -n "${MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME:-}" ]; then exec mongorestore --drop --archive -u "$MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_USERNAME" -p "$MONGO_INITDB_ROOT_PASSWORD" --authenticationDatabase admin fi exec mongorestore --drop --archive ' < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.archive" echo "mongorestore OK"
